oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Born to Weave
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Krueger, Ellen Feinstein
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 50 Number 3 (July 2023).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.in her death, she continued to enrich my life, gifting me with beautiful friendships with so many amazing people whom I would most likely have not otherwise met. Having each of you in my life has helped me wrestle with my grief by refocusing Laney’s passion and energy to make positive change as we move forward saving her beloved birds in the wild. I cannot thank all our incredible supporters enough! I am so appreciative of the support of Georgia Hayes and Bird Endowment’s board members, Lewis “Buddy” Waskey, Gen Anderson, Ray Varella, Carol Woodson, and Bennett Hennessey, each of whom knew Laney and were great champions of her work. I remember when I talked to Gen for the first time, she described Laney as the “perfect bridge” who united people from different parts of the ‘bird world’ in support of this amazing project. Thank you again and again! None of this would have happened without...Continued from page 17 September 1992 when she acquired her first pair of Blue-throated Macaws. In Laney’s own words, “Back then, there were almost no wildcaught Blue-throated Macaws offered for sale. When birds did become available, the price was not inconsiderable. But I knew I had to buy this pair. So, Ira was named after the IRA I raided and Eureka for the exhilaration of my achievement. I simply knew that it was my destiny to work with Blue-throated Macaws.” This wild-caught pair had been legally imported in 1982, and the birds were of an undetermined age; they had been together for 10 years, but never attempted to nest. Leave it to Laney! One year later, in August 1993, they fledged their first chicks and became the Blues Conservatory Founder Pair #1. She was hooked! I was always so proud of her and cheered her on when she and Jack “flew the coop,” leaving their careers in the...SUPPORT “SAVING THE BLUES”! There are multiple ways to assist in “Saving the Blues”! We would be delighted to have your support whether you wish to sponsor a Nido Adoptivo nest box, contribute toward the Habitat Restoration Project on the Laney Rickman Reserve, donate to the Laney Rickman Blue-throated Macaw Fund, or give a general donation of any amount. Thanks to your help, the Blue-throated Macaw is flying toward a brighter future!(Use the QR code for descriptionsVisit the digital issue of Watchbird for additional picturesClick the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 50 No. 3 (2023): Watchbird July 2023; 22-24
